    Home › Manufacturers › De Havilland Canada

    De Havilland Canada

    Canada · Founded 1928

    Bush planes, short fields, and the fastest turboprop airliner flying.

    Aircraft here1
    Longest32.83 m
    Combined built640
    First flights1998–1998
    The company

    The story so far

    De Havilland Canada built its reputation on aircraft that could operate where nothing else could — the Beaver, the Otter, the Twin Otter — rugged short-field designs that opened up the Canadian north and are still working around the world decades later.

    The Dash 8 carried that philosophy into the airline world, and the Dash 8-400 remains the fastest turboprop airliner in service. The company was absorbed into Bombardier, then re-established under its own name in 2019 — a rare case of an aviation marque coming back from the dead.

    The line-up

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
